research_question: Under a tractable natural training workload, does a topology-complete training-run transaction detect and prevent silent run-identity, recovery, and checkpoint-selection failures that simpler logging and weight-only checkpoint baselines miss, without unacceptable time-to-quality, resource, and operator cost?
unit_of_analysis: one prospectively registered run attempt, including failures and restarts

required_properties: enough steps to expose optimizer and scheduler state
fault injection without protected flagship outcome access
workload.selection_reason: A competent attempt must be tractable locally yet preserve the state, topology, failure, and selection mechanisms under test. A toy record-only result cannot adjudicate run integrity.

arms.name: single_process_reference
arms.purpose: Strong uninterrupted reference with the same model, data, objective, optimizer, schedule, budget, and evaluation.

arms.name: distributed_standard_logging
arms.purpose: Matched distributed baseline with framework-standard logging and checkpointing.

arms.name: distributed_weight_only_recovery
arms.purpose: Deliberately incomplete recovery control that retains weights but omits at least optimizer, scheduler, RNG, scaler, or data-cursor state.

arms.name: distributed_full_state_transaction
arms.purpose: Full governed transaction with frozen topology and numerics, complete state, run denominator, fault ledger, resume probes, checkpoint-family custody, and independent qualification.

arms.name: compiler_or_alternative_topology
arms.purpose: Matched competing topology method where supported, with equal tuning opportunity and explicit plan identity.

competence_gates: single-process reference reaches the frozen target
distributed baseline reaches a comparable target without injected faults
fault injector is demonstrated against positive controls
full-state inventory is framework- and workload-complete
checkpoint commit and corruption probes are independently checked
resume comparator distinguishes bitwise, operation-order-bounded, and statistical equivalence
data order and example multiplicity are externally reconstructed
all arms receive matched tuning and recovery opportunity
qualification remains unopened until selection freezes
failed runs and excluded runs remain in the denominator
results are repeated across at least three seeds and more than one fault timing
claims remain within model, scale, hardware, framework, and fault envelope
nonclaims: model quality outside the frozen target
exact resume where only statistical equivalence is tested
